name: Passover Strawberry-Kiwi Sponge-Cake Roll
recipeInstructions:
To make sponge cake roll: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 10x15-inch jelly roll pan and cover with greased wax paper.
1. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with an electric mixer until soft peaks form.
2. Gradually add 1/3 cup granulated sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
3. In a small bowl, beat egg yolks with an electric mixer until thick and lemon-colored (about 5 minutes).
4. Gradually add the remaining 1/3 cup of sugar, beating constantly.
5. Stir in lemon juice. Gently fold the yolk mixture into the egg white mixture.
6. Sift together potato starch, matzo cake meal, and salt.
7. Fold the dry ingredients, half at a time, into the egg mixture until just blended.
8. Spread batter evenly in jelly-roll pan and bake 15 minutes or until cake tests done (a wooden toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean).
9. Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes. Turn out onto a clean, cotton kitchen towel sprinkled with 1/3 cup confectioners sugar.
10. Roll up the cake and twirl together from the long end. Let cool completely.
To make fruit filling: Place 1 cup non-dairy topping in a bowl (reserve 1 cup for icing) and fold in sliced strawberries and kiwi.
To assemble: Unroll sponge cake and spread fruit-cream filling evenly over the cake. Roll the cake very carefully on an ungreased cookie sheet, seam side down. Cover with clear wrap and refrigerate. Just before serving, ice sides of filled cake with reserved topping. Garnish with sliced kiwis, whole strawberries, and mint leaves.
